text.title: Outcome Decomposition
text.summary: Rules for decomposing a business goal into product outcomes, leading indicators, and experiments — the core PM skill that connects roadmap items to measurable business impact.

rule.statement: Decompose business goals in this chain: Business Goal → Product Outcome → Leading Indicator → Experiment. Each layer must be causally plausible, not just correlated.
rule.rationale: Skipping layers produces roadmaps where features connect loosely to goals. The chain forces the team to articulate the mechanism: "If we improve onboarding (product outcome), TTV drops (leading indicator), which should reduce churn (business goal)."

rule.statement: Define success criteria for each experiment before running it. Statistical significance required for experiments with 1000+ users; practical significance threshold required for smaller samples.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc success criteria are subject to confirmation bias. Pre-defined criteria make the experiment falsifiable.

example.note: Business goal "increase revenue 20%" decomposed into two product outcomes, each with leading indicators and experiments.
example.code:
Business Goal: Increase revenue 20%
Product Outcome 1: Reduce churn from 8% to 5% Leading Indicator: NPS improvement (proxy for retention intent) Experiments: - Onboarding redesign (hypothesis: reduces activation time by 40%) - Re-engagement email sequence (hypothesis: 15% win-back rate) Success Criteria: defined upfront, statistical significance at p<0.05
Product Outcome 2: Increase activation rate from 25% to 40% Leading Indicator: Time-to-value (TTV) reduction Experiments: - Guided tour for new users - Personalized feature suggestions based on role Success Criteria: TTV from 3 days to 1 day, measured on cohorts

rule.statement: Present yourself as "architect of impact," not "owner of the roadmap." The roadmap is the artifact; the outcome is the job.
rule.rationale: PMs who identify as roadmap owners defend features. PMs who identify as outcome architects drop features that are not moving the metric and replace them — which is the right behavior.
